titleStmt.title: Letter from James Davidson to Clara Davidson, 15 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: James Davidson

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from James Davidson (1877-1916) to his mother, Clara Mary (b.1852). James writes of life at the front, detailing casualties sustained since he last contacted her. He writes that the weather is cold, wet and miserable. He sends thanks to his father and sister for writing and requests some more supplies.
This letter is part of a large correspondence from Captain James Davidson to his parents. Davidson, who was Managing Director of the Sirocco Engineering Works in Belfast, served with 13 Royal Irish Rifles and the Machine Gun Corps. His letters describe his experiences while training in England and serving in France. He was killed on 1 July 1916, the first day of the Battle of the Somme.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
June 15, 1916 My dearest Mother Have not
written you for some
days for I know surley
had a minute to
myself & now there
is only just time
to send you a
hurried line before
post goes out. I have lost 1 killed
&: 7 wounded in
the last week from
which you can
to some extent realise
what things have been
like. I don't want
you to worry mother
dear if you have
no more letters from
me for some little
time. There are
several things ahead
about which I can't
say more but I
sall be all right.
Am feeling quite fit
again but the weather
is really horrible -
nothing but rain &:
storm & and so cold.
Surely there must be
a change soon.
Farther down mother
for just letters &
would you also
thank May & Kathleen
for theirs & Father
for his written from
works. Parcels are all arriving
regularly. Would
you please send me
a tube of Holygrass &
shaving cream. Post man is up
so must close -
There is so much to
be done in the next
few days that I
scarcely know what
to do first. my fondesnt love to
all
your loving son James.
On Active Service Written 15th June Wed 1916 Mrs S C Davidson
Seacourt
Bangor
CoDown Ireland MDavidson 15 June 1916
